text.title: inverted-header-content-first Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules for inverted-header schemas.

rule.statement: In any single SO call producing both content and metadata about that content, the content field MUST be declared first and every metadata field after it.
rule.rationale: Castillo measured 46.67% vs 33.33% LiveBench accuracy (p < 0.01) for reasoning-before-answer vs answer-before-reasoning; AWS Bedrock 2026 SO design guide calls field order "Chain of Thought embedded in the data structure".

rule.statement: If a streaming UI requires the title before the body completes, the pipeline MUST split into two SO calls (body first, then a cheap metadata call); it MUST NOT reorder the schema to put title first.
rule.rationale: Reordering trades measurable accuracy loss for UI convenience that two calls solve cleanly.

rule.statement: If the title is a hard input constraint provided by the user, it MUST be passed in the prompt and OMITTED from the schema.
rule.rationale: Generating a title that was already supplied wastes tokens and risks drift from the user's intent.

rule.statement: Each metadata field's `description` MUST reference the body field by backtick-wrapped name (e.g. "Sharp 6-10 word title derived from `body` and `summary`").
rule.rationale: Naming the dependency re-grounds the model on what it is summarising and prevents drift.
